intro: Dolichochaete bequaertii, also known as the Philippine golden chain, is a climbing plant species belonging to the genus Dolichochaete. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dolichochaete bequaertii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. In herbal medicine, Dolichochaete bequaertii is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
